data.description:
## Why
Turn open-ended assumption checking into an enumerable, probe-each scan, and add a cheap early hedge signal -- all feeding WS1s committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim.

## What
- G-7(a): give E4 a confident-assertion trigger lexicon as its SCAN PROTOCOL (enumerate assertion-shaped sentences, probe each via Grep/Read) instead of open-ended "verify assumptions".
- G-4: E4 sub-check for scope-exclusion / descoping claims ("already exists", "handled by Y", "out of scope because Z"). NORMAL priority (do NOT elevate to highest -- avoid distorting existing E4 priorities). Scoped to empirically-checkable (codebase) exclusions; abstain-with-coverage for external-fact exclusions.
- G-7(b): exec:DET hedge-word detector ("probably", "assume", "should be", "seems") emitting findings that flow through Pass 2. DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges (which already does LLM-tier hedge detection) -- this is the cheaper/earlier DET signal, not a duplicate. The verifier judges SUBSTANCE (committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim), not word presence, so mis-flags get low validity -> dropped.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E4 confident-assertion scan-protocol lexicon
- [ ] E4 scope-exclusion sub-check at normal priority
- [ ] exec:DET hedge detector, deduped vs E6.no_hedges, routed to WS1 substance sub-answer
- [ ] Nits (rhetorical hedges, well-formed assumptions with fallback) verified as dropped
data.title: WS2: E4 detectors + exec:DET hedge detector feeding the shared sub-answer
